title: Somewhat Tameable Spiders
description: Use honey bottles to befriend spiders. Become your own spider jockey!
body:
Did you know that ocelots can't be tamed, but have a trusting mechanic instead? This mod allows something similar for base game spiders, and all that could entail. Turns out they weren't evil, just hungry!

## Trusting

**Approach a spider with a honey bottle** to tempt them. They'll even stop mid-attack if you haven't struck them yet! Then keep feeding their favourite bug-themed treat until hearts pop out.

Trusting spiders do not despawn and will not attack players in darkness, while still retaliating if hurt. This will not have them lose their trust when hurt though.

## Husbandry

You cannot breed spiders, trusting or not. However, you can leash them, and also heal any damage they've taken with a piece of raw meat. Rotten meat too.

## Mounts

If they seem big enough to hold a player's weight (for a regular sized human player, sorry tiny Origins), or in other words not cave spiders, you can **right-click a spider with a saddle** to have them wear it.

Yes. You can be your own spider jockey. Spiders are a decently fast one-person mount that can climb up and slide down vertical surfaces, and as such are very suited for exploration. 

Be sure to avoid low ceilings though, as spiders do not ultimately care if you crush your skull. 

Also, for one's sanity, spiders are immune to fall damage while being ridden by players.

## Customization

### Configurations

TBD.

### Data and resource packs

Items on the tag `somewhat-tameable-spiders:spider_food` heal trusting spiders. By default, it uses `c:foods/raw_meat` so it should work out of the game in any modpack that adds food items.

The spider saddle texture, an overlay over the base model, is located in `assets/somewhat-tameable-spiders/textures/entity/spider/spider_saddle.png`.

## Compatibility

- [No Hostiles Around Campfire](https://modrinth.com/mod/no-hostiles-around-campfire): Trusting spiders do not count as hostile mobs
